constituents: Diporidium fascicularis, also known as Devil's claw, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active constituents of Diporidium fascicularis are believed to include iridoid glycosides, particularly harpagoside, which is responsible for its medicinal effects. Harpagoside has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it a popular natural remedy for conditions such as arthritis and fibromyalgia.
